title: PowerShell Objects & Pipelines
description: Learn how PowerShell passes objects through pipelines using Format, Select, Sort, Measure, and Get-Member.

tasks.prompt: List all processes and format the output as a table.
tasks.explanation: Format-Table renders object properties in a readable column layout.

tasks.prompt: List all services and select only the Name and Status properties.
tasks.hint: Pipe Get-Service to Select-Object with property names.
tasks.explanation: Select-Object narrows output to specific properties from pipeline objects.

tasks.prompt: List processes sorted by CPU usage in descending order.
tasks.explanation: Sort-Object orders pipeline objects by one or more property values.

tasks.prompt: Count the total number of registered services.
tasks.explanation: Measure-Object returns aggregate statistics such as Count for a collection.

tasks.prompt: Show only the first 5 processes from the process list.
tasks.explanation: Select-Object -First limits how many objects pass through the pipeline.

tasks.prompt: Inspect the members (properties and methods) of process objects.
tasks.explanation: Get-Member reveals the type, properties, and methods available on objects.

tasks.prompt: Group files in the current directory by their file extension.
tasks.hint: Pipe Get-ChildItem to Group-Object using the Extension property.
tasks.explanation: Group-Object clusters objects that share the same property value.

tasks.prompt: Display the current date and time formatted as a detailed list.
tasks.explanation: Format-List shows each property on its own line for detailed inspection.
